title: Modern workstation hardware: NVMe, GPU compute and Apple silicon
paras: Multimedia workstations have shifted from spinning disks and optical drives to NVMe SSDs on the PCIe bus, high-core-count CPUs and powerful GPUs that accelerate encoding, effects and AI. Apple's M-series (Apple silicon) integrates CPU, GPU and media engines on one chip, and hardware encoders/decoders (NVENC, QuickSync) offload video work from the CPU.

title: Networking and the cloud today
paras: Local networks now run Gigabit/10-Gigabit Ethernet and Wi-Fi 6/6E, and fibre to the premises is common. Storage and collaboration have moved to the cloud (object storage, shared drives) and to on-prem NAS, replacing the removable media and simple LANs described in the book.

title: AI in image and media tools
paras: Image and video applications now include AI/ML features as standard: generative fill and object removal, upscaling and denoising, background removal, auto-masking and rotoscoping, and speech-to-text captioning. These accelerate tasks that were manual when the book was written.

title: Optical discs and floppy media
paras: CD/DVD/Blu-ray burners, floppy diskettes and other removable optical/magnetic media are effectively obsolete for production. Modern machines often ship without an optical drive; distribution is by network, SSD or the cloud.
